response.overview: "Hammering Out (an old argument)" is a video projection that unfolds on a white wall, embodying a rhythmic and relentless act of destruction. The work, lasting approximately 30 minutes, is edited into four non-repeating loops, akin to the structure of a musical composition. This arrangement transforms the video into a continuous, never-ending sequence, defying the conventional expectations of a narrative arc and instead creating a choreography of (de)construction.
response.title: Hammering Out (an old argument)
